claim: EVA 'm' (1,055 tokens) and 'g' (127 tokens) are suffix/final-marker glyphs, extending the suffix class beyond {y,n,l,r} per H023. Final-position rates: m 93.6%, g 83.5%. EVA 'l' (previously assumed final-dominant) is actually balanced (53.6% final / 32.6% mid) and should be demoted from the suffix class.
evidence_for: n at 97.0% final, y at 84.3% final, r at 74.4% final (previously known)
l at 53.6% final is a mis-assignment in H023
evidence_against: g's sample size (127) is modest; rate could shift with more data
Suffix class membership is a surface feature, not guaranteed to be grammatical suffixes
test_threshold: m >=90% final AND g >=80% final: confirmed

tests_run.details: Expands H023 suffix class to {y,n,r,m,g}; demotes l. Any stem-stripping pipeline should be updated.

reasoning: Expands H023 suffix class to {y,n,r,m,g}; demotes l. Any stem-stripping pipeline should be updated.
